anchorText: Gluttony, Society's Most Acceptable Sin
glorify God in your body and in your spirit

nodeData.topic: 과식과 자기 절제: 몸을 통해 하나님께 영광을 돌리는 삶

anchorText: Overindulgence is a hallmark of our modern American culture.
We are continuing this series called White Collar Sins.

children.topic: 1. 과식이 현대 문화와 교회 안에서 너무 쉽게 정상화되는 현실

anchorText: Overindulgence is a hallmark of our modern American culture.
The temptation to eat and drink too much or indulge in any number of things is constant.

children.topic: 과식은 현대 미국 문화의 특징이며, 음식과 술뿐 아니라 여러 영역으로 확장된다

anchorText: Overindulgence is a hallmark of our modern American culture.
The temptation to eat and drink too much or indulge in any number of things is constant. It's also trivialized.

children.topic: 현대 문화의 지속적인 유혹과 가벼운 취급

anchorText: The basic idea of gluttony is over indulgence.
We also need to broaden that out a bit and think of over-consumption, over consumerism, over indulgence in just about anything.

children.topic: 과식은 단지 음식 문제가 아니라 과소비와 전반적 절제로 확장된다

anchorText: Christians are quick to judge others on obvious blatant sins.
they themselves, while they say that, may be guilty of this very sin of gluttony.

children.topic: 기독교인도 눈에 띄는 죄는 빨리 정죄하지만 같은 과식에는 둔감할 수 있다

anchorText: It was reported that Spurgeon even said Mr. Moody, I'll put down my cigars when you put down your fork.
Christians are quick to judge others on obvious blatant sins.

children.topic: 스펄전과 무디의 일화는 다른 죄를 지적하기 전에 자기 자신을 돌아보게 한다

anchorText: You've probably never heard a sermon on gluttony in your life.
Five Reasons Why Pastors Don't Preach on Gluttony.

children.topic: 과식 설교가 거의 없었던 이유와, 그럼에도 설교해야 하는 이유

anchorText: The Bible does speak about this issue in Proverbs 23.
In Ezekiel Chapter 16, there's a very interesting text regarding the city of Sodom.

children.topic: 성경은 이미 오래전부터 과식과 절제의 문제를 다루어 왔다

anchorText: do not mix with wine bibbers or gluttonous eaters of meat, for both will come to poverty.

children.topic: 잠언은 술주정과 고기 탐식을 함께 경고한다

anchorText: the sins of Sodom were pride, laziness, and gluttony, while the poor and the needy suffered outside.

children.topic: 에스겔은 소돔의 죄 가운데 교만, 게으름, 과식을 함께 언급한다

anchorText: your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit
For you were bought at a price.

children.topic: 2. 고린도전서 6장으로 보는 몸의 신학: 몸은 성령의 전이다

anchorText: your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you
your body is the temple

children.topic: 몸은 거룩한 장소이며, 고린도의 쾌락 문화와는 다른 목적을 가진다

anchorText: your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God, and you are not your own?
Paul is speaking here about the physical human body.

children.topic: 바울은 몸을 성전으로 부르며, 개인의 육체를 다루고 있다

anchorText: food for the stomach and the stomach for foods.
They used it to justify gluttony, sexual immorality, drunkenness.

children.topic: 고린도 사람들의 구호는 욕망을 정당화하는 쾌락주의의 언어였다

anchorText: your body is the temple
So your body is a holy place.

children.topic: 성전 비유는 몸을 깨끗이 관리해야 한다는 뜻을 담고 있다

anchorText: A temple was a place of worship. The temple was a place of prayer.
they would maintain and clean that temple.

children.topic: 성전은 예배와 기도와 희생의 자리였고 늘 관리되었다

anchorText: Jesus, twice in his ministry-- we call it "cleansed" the temple.
I see my body is a temple.

children.topic: 예수님이 성전을 깨끗하게 하신 것처럼 몸도 정결하게 보아야 한다

anchorText: Your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit, who is in you
your body has a holy person

children.topic: 몸 안에 거하시는 성령 때문에 몸은 거룩한 인격의 거처가 된다

anchorText: Your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit, who is in you, and whom you have from God.
He takes up residence in your body.

children.topic: 이방 신전과 달리 그리스도인의 몸 안에는 성령이 실제로 거하신다

anchorText: your body as the base of operations from which the Holy Spirit works
Holy Spirit's headquarters right now in my physical body.

children.topic: 몸은 성령의 본부가 되어 사람들을 향한 사역의 거점이 된다

anchorText: you were bought at a price.
you are not your own.

children.topic: 몸은 값으로 산 것이며, 그러므로 하나님께 속한 삶을 살아야 한다

anchorText: you were bought at a price.
you cannot claim independence.

children.topic: 그리스도의 보혈로 값 주고 사신 존재이므로 독립을 주장할 수 없다

anchorText: Wouldn't you want to make sure that the base of operations for the Holy Spirit, your body, is kept as pure as possible?

children.topic: 값비싼 대가가 있었기에 몸을 가능한 한 순전하게 지켜야 한다

anchorText: Why do people become gluttons
a flawed view

children.topic: 3. 과식의 뿌리와 그 유혹

anchorText: whose God is their belly
they worship their appetites

children.topic: 1. 욕망: 과식은 배를 신처럼 섬기며 욕망에 지배당하는 삶에서 나온다

anchorText: their primary view of humanity is I exist here for my pleasure.
Epicurus and his followers ... life's sole purpose was for enjoyment

children.topic: 사람들은 삶의 목적을 쾌락으로 착각하고 음식과 음료를 과도하게 추구한다

anchorText: Whose God is their belly and who set their mind on earthly things.
Their appetites control them.

children.topic: 배가 신이 되면 욕망이 사람을 다스리고 사람은 욕망을 섬기게 된다

anchorText: Gluttony is often regarded as an emotional cry for help.
something is eating us.

children.topic: 2. 상처: 과식은 감정적 상처를 덮으려는 도피가 되기도 한다

anchorText: gluttony is an emotional escape, a sign that something is eating us.
Gluttony is often regarded as an emotional cry for help.

children.topic: 먹는 행위는 감정적 압박에서 벗어나려는 울부짖음이 될 수 있다

anchorText: He admitted to eating to seek relief from emotional pressure.
He died of a heart attack at age 28, most articles will tell you directly due to obesity.

children.topic: 빅 펀과 말론 브란도는 과식이 실제로 건강과 생명을 무너뜨린 사례다

the stomach might be full while the heart remains empty

anchorText: Satan often tempts people with food.
command these stones to be turned into bread.

anchorText: the stomach might be full while the heart remains empty.
a God sized hole can only be filled with God himself.

children.topic: 배는 불러도 마음은 비어 있을 수 있으며, 하나님 모양의 빈자리는 하나님만 채운다

anchorText: How does one overcome gluttony?
Therefore, glorify God in your body and in your spirit

children.topic: 4. 과식을 이기는 길

develop an appetite for truth

anchorText: oh taste and see that the Lord is good.
desire the pure milk of the word that you may grow thereby.

children.topic: 성경은 진리를 맛보는 것과 말씀의 순수한 젖을 사모하는 것으로 표현된다

anchorText: man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds from the mouth of God.

anchorText: we need to say no
saying no is self-control

anchorText: he purposed in his heart not to defile himself with a portion of the King's delicacies.
Daniel said no.

anchorText: when you fast, you are making your appetite your slave.
I'm going to control you so that for a period of time I can focus on the Lord's voice.

children.topic: 금식은 식욕을 종으로 두고 주님의 음성에 집중하게 한다

anchorText: we need to say go
bodily exercise profits a little

children.topic: 몸을 움직이고 건강하게 관리하여 하나님 손에 쓰임받는 도구로 지켜라

anchorText: get up and move that body around.
make sure that your body is a well tuned instrument in God's hands.

children.topic: 몸은 오랫동안 유용하게 쓰일 수 있도록 훈련하고 관리해야 한다

anchorText: I do it because I see it as a way to tell God thank you for the body you've given me.
I'm a steward of what he's given me.

children.topic: 몸을 돌보는 일은 하나님께 대한 감사의 표현이다

anchorText: therefore, glorify God in your body
your body should be a billboard to the glory of God

children.topic: 결국 몸은 하나님의 영광을 드러내는 빌보드가 되어야 한다

anchorText: my life for His glory.
When I live for his pleasure and his purpose, I find pleasure and I find my purpose.

children.topic: 하나님을 위해 사는 삶은 오히려 기쁨과 목적을 더 크게 경험하게 한다

anchorText: Your hands can help the needy. Your feet can go to the lonely.
you are telling a world God cares about you. God loves you.

children.topic: 말과 손과 발과 눈을 통해 하나님이 사람을 사랑하신다는 것을 보여 주라
